title: Will AI destroy us... or save us?
description: Like it or not, artificial intelligence is deeply rooted in our lives. Its invisible architecture stretches everywhere from dating apps to medical care. In this new world, what remains uniquely human? On today's episode, we explore the tension between our love of AI and our fear of it — and try to decode the humans behind the machines. This episode originally published in March of 2023.Guests:George Zarkadakis, author of In Our Own Image: Will Artificial Intelligence Save or Destroy Us?Francis Collins, physician-geneticist who led the Human Genome ProjectStephanie Dick, assistant professor in the School of Communication at Simon Fraser UniversityMeredith Broussard, data journalism professor at New York University, and author of More Than a Glitch: Confronting Race, Gender and Ability Bias in TechTo access bonus episodes and listen to Throughline sponsor-free, subscribe to Throughline+ via Apple Podcasts or at plus.npr.org/throughline.

title: Arm CEO Haas in line to lead much of parent SoftBank's international business: Report
description: Arm's CEO, Rene Haas, is set to head a significant portion of SoftBank's international operations. This move is aimed at bolstering SoftBank's ambitious AI chip strategy, known as Project Izanagi. Haas will also continue his leadership role at Arm. This development comes as SoftBank founder Masayoshi Son intensifies his focus on artificial intelligence investments.

title: Bengaluru’s biggest AI leadership event
description: As artificial intelligence reshapes the foundations of knowledge work, the ET Future of Knowledge Work Summit in Bengaluru brings together India’s leading CEOs, CIOs, policymakers, and AI practitioners to explore how organisations are transforming productivity, decision-making, and workforce structures in real time.
content:
Synopsis
As AI reshapes how work gets done, leaders must act with clarity, not theory. The ET Future of Knowledge Work Summit brings India’s top decision-makers together to decode what’s changing inside organisations today, and how to lead through it... [5587 chars]

title: Gen Z's souring views on artificial intelligence
description: A recent survey shows that Gen Z's feelings about artificial intelligence are becoming increasingly negative despite regular use. Read more at straitstimes.com.
content:
NEW YORK – Think young people are charging eagerly into an AI-mediated future? Think again.
